topic.what: Impact of differential privacy mechanisms on Cox proportional hazards model statistical utility
topic.how: Multi-dataset simulation with 5 clinical datasets (n=168-6524), 15 epsilon levels (0.1-1000), 3 input perturbation approaches vs output perturbation, 1000 Monte Carlo iterations
topic.why_matters: Clinical survival analysis increasingly requires privacy protection; systematic evaluation of DP impact on inference and prediction is lacking, creating decision uncertainty for medical researchers

characteristics.what_it_measures: Proportion of baseline-significant covariates losing significance after DP perturbation
characteristics.evidence: Table S1, Fig 1: LSR at epsilon=1 ranged 0.899-0.944 across all 5 datasets
characteristics.context: Phase 1 covariate-only perturbation; even largest dataset (n=6524) shows 89.9% LSR

characteristics.evidence: Table S2, Fig 2: test C-index collapses to random levels at epsilon<=1
Table IV: rotterdam=10, colon=30, pbc=60, lung=60, flchain=30
characteristics.context: Phase 1 covariate-only perturbation; varies by n, q, and covariate range

characteristics.evidence: Table IV: flchain=5, colon=10, rotterdam=10, lung=7, pbc=30
characteristics.context: Phase 1; output perturbation requires only epsilon=0.5-7

characteristics.what_it_measures: Proportion of baseline-nonsignificant covariates becoming significant after DP
characteristics.value: Peaks 93-100% at epsilon=100 for near-threshold variables
characteristics.evidence: Fig 3-4: FPR increases at moderate-high epsilon (10-100) for variables with p near 0.05
characteristics.context: Phase 1-3; FPR danger zone overlaps with significance recovery zone

characteristics.what_it_measures: Bias in hazard ratio estimates from DP noise
characteristics.evidence: Fig 5-6: effect sizes vanish at low epsilon; cannot discern effect direction
characteristics.context: Phase 1; bias remains significant at epsilon<=5 even for large datasets

characteristics.what_it_measures: Utility difference between input perturbation (Local DP) and output perturbation (Central DP)
characteristics.value: Output perturbation outperforms Phase 1 at all epsilon for 4/5 datasets
characteristics.evidence: Fig 2, Table S2: dfbeta maintains near-baseline C-index at epsilon>=5; reflects theoretical sqrt(n) gap
characteristics.context: Structural difference due to trust model; Local DP has stronger privacy guarantees

novelty: First systematic evaluation of 3 input perturbation approaches vs output perturbation across 5 datasets x 15 epsilon
Direct quantification of inferential utility loss via loss-of-significance-rate
Elucidation of scaling effect in transition zone epsilon=3-10
Practical epsilon threshold guidelines for medical researchers
Comprehensive false-positive risk characterization at moderate-high epsilon
open_problems: Formal DP guarantees require data-independent bounds; current results are optimistic lower bounds
Large-scale validation (n>10000) not verified; extrapolation relies on theory
Smooth sensitivity or propose-test-release for tighter formal bounds
Extension to federated learning settings with DP
